mission.title: flux: Reduce the mean time to recovery (MTTR) in case of failed deployments
mission.description: Reduce the mean time to recovery (MTTR) in case of failed deployments. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
In Flux 2.7 we've added a new feature to kustomize-controller to speed up the cluster reconciliation by canceling ongoing health checks when a new source revision is detected.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Reduce the mean time to recovery (MTTR) in case of failed…
steps.description:
Helm support is now implemented [here](https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/31435). It will land in Helm 4 in December, so we can target support for both kustomize and helm in Flux 2.8.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Reduce the mean time to recovery (MTTR) in case of failed…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Helm support is now implemented [here](https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/31435). It will land in Helm 4 in December, so we can target support for both kustomize and helm in Flux 2.8.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
